info.title: Sakshi Agent Governance API
info.description: Registry (KYA), decision flight recorder, bounded autonomy, evidence packs, fairness screens. Auth: bearer ingest keys create evidence; OIDC JWTs with governance roles mutate state.

get.description:
Public status: liveness + version only — never data, never
chain state (those need a principal).

schema.title: Response Meta Api V1 Register  Meta Get
Response List Agents Api V1 Agents Get

post.description:
Attach an external identity (#68): verify an A2A Signed Agent Card
(Ed25519 over the card bytes) or record a declared identity (Entra
Agent ID / workload identity — a claim, not proof). The verdict is
attested; the agent carries its verification status.

schema.title: Response Attach Identity Api V1 Agents  Agent Id  Identity Post

get.description:
Agents with no accountable owner. Design doc 2.1: no orphans — these
get an owner within a week or get suspended.

schema.title: Response List Orphans Api V1 Agents Orphans Get
Response List Provenance Api V1 Agents  Agent Id  Provenance Get
Response List Models Api V1 Models Get
Response List Attestations Api V1 Attestations Get

post.description:
Mint a managed ingest key. The raw value appears in THIS response and
never again — only its SHA-256 is stored. Minting is itself evidence.

schema.title: Response Mint Api Key Api V1 Api Keys Post
Response List Api Keys Api V1 Api Keys Get

post.description:
Revocation takes effect on the next request — the auth layer reads
the row, there is no cache to flush. Also evidence.

schema.title: Response Revoke Api Key Api V1 Api Keys  Key Id  Revoke Post

post.description:
Register a webhook endpoint. The signing secret appears in THIS
response; receivers verify X-Sakshi-Signature with it. Registration is
itself evidence (attested), like ingest keys.

schema.title: Response Create Webhook Api V1 Webhooks Post
Response List Webhooks Api V1 Webhooks Get

post.description:
Send a signed test event to a webhook so the receiver can verify
wiring + signature before a real governance event fires.

schema.title: Response Test Webhook Api V1 Webhooks  Webhook Id  Test Post
Response Revoke Webhook Api V1 Webhooks  Webhook Id  Revoke Post
Response Meta Api V1 Witness  Meta Get

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Prefix search over client_ref or record hash
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Prefix search over client_ref or record hash

schema.title: Response List Decisions Api V1 Decisions Get

get.description:
Recompute every hash in the range and check linkage. A regulator, an
auditor, or the customer can run this without trusting us.

schema.title: Response Meta Api V1 Bound  Meta Get

post.description:
Formally lint an envelope spec WITHOUT publishing (#72) — check for
dead rules, escalation-monotonicity violations, and default reach
before it governs anything.

schema.title: Response Lint Envelope Spec Api V1 Bound Lint Envelope Post

post.description:
New envelope version; prior versions deactivate but are never deleted —
the envelope in force at any past decision stays reconstructable.

get.description: Formal-lint verdict for the agent's ACTIVE envelope (#72).

schema.title: Response Get Active Envelope Lint Api V1 Bound Agents  Agent Id  Envelope Lint Get
Response List Envelope Versions Api V1 Bound Agents  Agent Id  Envelope Versions Get

post.description:
Route one intended action through the agent's active envelope. Every
call is persisted — routing itself is evidence (paras 60–63).

Customer-triggered human handoff (#65, RBI draft 59(iii)): the agent
runtime calls this when a customer asks for a human. Creates a HANDOFF
queue item in the agent's org unit — a review a customer requested is
still a review, worked in the same queues, and its existence is the
disclosure-control evidence.

schema.title: Response Request Handoff Api V1 Bound Handoff Post

get.description: Per-agent token usage + cost (when priced) + incident MTTR (#57).

schema.title: Response Telemetry Usage Api V1 Bound Telemetry Usage Get

get.description:
Override rates, time-on-task, escalation and deep-review disagreement
per reviewer and per agent — the anti-rubber-stamp evidence (paras 61-63).

schema.title: Response Telemetry Api V1 Bound Telemetry Get

get.description: The SDK's polling endpoint (#25): cheap, cacheable, no side effects.

schema.title: Response List Kill Events Api V1 Bound Kill Events Get

post.description:
Assemble the drill attestation from the event timeline: activation,
what was blocked while halted, release. Para 43/60(ii) evidence — an
untested kill switch is a compliance claim, not a control.

schema.title: Response Attest Drill Api V1 Bound Kill Events  Activation Id  Attestation Post

get.description:
The configured policy (if any) plus the effective response — absent a
policy, blast radius decides, and the caller should see that too.

schema.title: Response Get Response Policy Api V1 Bound Agents  Agent Id  Response Policy Get

put.description:
Append-only like envelopes: a change is a new version. Provenance pin
is deliberately absent — that is a human recovery decision made during
the investigation, never a standing automatic response.

schema.title: Response List Breakers Api V1 Bound Agents  Agent Id  Breakers Get

post.description:
Evaluate enabled breakers now; any that trip fire the SAME kill path
with actor 'breaker:<name>'. Also run periodically in-process — this
endpoint exists for drills, tests, and external schedulers.

schema.title: Response Run Breaker Check Api V1 Bound Breakers Check Post
Response List Reviews Api V1 Bound Reviews Get

post.description:
Marks when a reviewer actually started looking — time-on-task telemetry
(#20) measures claim->resolve, not create->resolve.

schema.title: Response List Evaluations Api V1 Bound Evaluations Get

get.description:
Fleet-wide safety posture in one view: every agent's halt state + its
armed circuit breakers (per-agent detail lives on the agent page). The
kill/drill LOG is /kill/events.

schema.title: Response Fleet Safety Api V1 Bound Safety Get
Response Meta Api V1 Vidhi  Meta Get

get.description:
Regulatory Readiness Score (#52): this deployment scored live against
each clause map. Adoption moves the score; assertions do not.

schema.title: Response Get Readiness Api V1 Vidhi Readiness Get
Response List Clause Maps Api V1 Vidhi Clause Maps Get
Response Get Clause Map Api V1 Vidhi Clause Maps  Name  Get

get.description:
Generate the evidence pack for a clause map, live: chain verification
runs, telemetry computes, counts query — never a cached document.

schema.title: Response Build Pack Api V1 Vidhi Packs  Map Name  Get

get.description:
The evidence bundle: a freshly generated pack, Ed25519-signed with
this deployment's key — verifiable offline by anyone with the public key.

schema.title: Response Export Pack Api V1 Vidhi Packs  Map Name  Export Get

post.description:
Declaring an incident is a human act (reviewer and above); the report
assembles itself from the flight recorder.

schema.title: Response List Incidents Api V1 Vidhi Incidents Get

get.description:
RTI-shaped disclosure pack for one decision (#59): point-wise
query/reply/source answers assembled from the flight recorder.

parameters.name: signed

schema.description: wrap in the Ed25519 evidence-bundle envelope
schema.title: Signed

parameters.description: wrap in the Ed25519 evidence-bundle envelope

schema.title: Response Rti Decision Api V1 Vidhi Rti Decision Get
Response Incident Report Api V1 Vidhi Incidents  Incident Id  Report Get

get.description: Signed incident bundle — same Ed25519 envelope as evidence packs.

schema.title: Response Incident Export Api V1 Vidhi Incidents  Incident Id  Export Get

post.description:
Store a citable eval run. Metrics are supplied (scored offline by
exact-match, the Indic judge, or humans — recorded in `scorer`); the
platform computes the GATE VERDICT against Wilson confidence intervals
and stores the exact artifact. Packs cite it by id as validation
evidence.

schema.title: Response Create Eval Run Api V1 Vidhi Eval Runs Post
Response List Eval Runs Api V1 Vidhi Eval Runs Get
Response Get Eval Run Api V1 Vidhi Eval Runs  Run Id  Get

get.description:
The algorithmic-processing sections of a DPDP Rule-13 DPIA,
pre-filled from live evidence.

schema.title: Response Dpia Support Api V1 Vidhi Dpia Get

get.description:
Independent-audit bundle (DPDP Rule 13(2)): DPIA substrate + every
regulator pack + readiness snapshot, in one signed artifact for the
auditor. Auditor-role principals (read-only) can pull this without
being able to change anything — the 'hand the auditor a login' story
extended to a portable, verifiable export.

schema.title: Response Audit Bundle Api V1 Vidhi Audit Bundle Get
Response Meta Api V1 Vishwas  Meta Get

get.description:
Inference-free disparity screen: decision outcomes by locality against
census SC/ST population shares (ETHICS.md §1a). Flags, never proves —
the caveats travel inside the payload.

schema.title: Response Get Locality Screen Api V1 Vishwas Locality Screen Get

get.description:
Declared-attribute fairness screen (#30) computed live off the flight
recorder — production-grounded monitoring, no inference.

schema.title: Response Get Declared Screen Api V1 Vishwas Declared Screen Get

post.description:
Pre-deploy bias testing: screen a batch of candidate decisions with
declared attributes BEFORE the agent goes live. The stored result is the
citable artifact; packs reference it by id.

schema.title: Response Create Predeploy Run Api V1 Vishwas Predeploy Runs Post
Response List Predeploy Runs Api V1 Vishwas Predeploy Runs Get

post.description:
Verify a storage disposition (#80 D2) — a signed vault receipt or a
reference key against the configured vault — without ever seeing the
raw value.

schema.title: Response Verify Disposition Endpoint Api V1 Vishwas Verify Disposition Post

get.description:
Document/KYC extraction governance (#80): data-minimization +
storage-disposition findings computed live off the flight recorder.

schema.title: Response Get Extraction Screen Api V1 Vishwas Extraction Screen Get

get.description:
MCP governance (#58): tool calls + manifests witnessed off the flight
recorder, and the tool-poisoning findings (mcp_manifest_changed).

schema.title: Response Get Mcp Screen Api V1 Vishwas Mcp Screen Get
Response Get Predeploy Run Api V1 Vishwas Predeploy Runs  Run Id  Get

get.description:
A scoped, grounded digest of what changed since this user was last
here and what needs them now — plus, when enabled, a sovereign-LLM
narration over ONLY those facts. The digest is authoritative.

parameters.name: narrative

schema.description: also generate the natural-language summary (the digest is always returned)
schema.title: Narrative

parameters.description: also generate the natural-language summary (the digest is always returned)

schema.title: Response Get Briefing Api V1 Me Briefing Get

get.description:
The caller's EFFECTIVE Sakshi entitlements after the access mapping is
applied (ADR-021) — the console reads this at sign-in so nav matches what
the API authorizes. Any authenticated user; reflects the resolved principal,
not the raw IdP claims.

schema.title: Response My Entitlements Api V1 Me Entitlements Get
Response List Rules Api V1 Admin Entitlement Rules Get

post.description:
Dry-run: given a sample user's raw claims, show the entitlements the
current ruleset would grant — the wizard's preview (Slice C uses this).

Dry-run against DRAFT (unsaved) rules — the wizard previews a sample user
before anything is written. No DB read; resolves the provided rules only.

get.description:
Distinct IdP role/group names seen from real logins — seeds the wizard so
an admin maps what their IdP actually sends, not a blank form. Names only.

Read-only view of the deployment's effective, NON-SECRET configuration —
so an admin can verify posture without shell access, and cite it as evidence.
Secret VALUES are never returned (only 'configured' / 'not'); these settings
are deploy-time env (SAKSHI_*), not runtime-editable here.

schema.title: Response Platform Posture Api V1 Admin Platform Get

get.description:
The attested log of access-control changes — every rule and toggle edit,
who made it and when. 'Who can approve' is change-controlled and evidenced.

schema.title: Response Access Activity Api V1 Admin Access Activity Get

deep_review_rate.title: Deep Review Rate
deep_review_rate.description: Fraction of auto-executed decisions sampled into deep review

scorer.title: Scorer
scorer.description: how metrics were produced: exact-match | judge-indic:<ver> | human-<n> | debate — provenance, not a claim we ran it

detail.title: Detail
detail.description: optional structured evidence beyond scalar metrics (e.g. the red-team MAP-Elites grid + diverse evasions)

data_source.title: Data Source
data_source.description: provenance of the batch (dataset name, extract date, system)

safe_envelope_id.title: Safe Envelope Id
safe_envelope_id.description: Designated conservative envelope for safe mode; omitted = everything routes sync_review

corrected_action.title: Corrected Action
corrected_action.description: Required when the verdict is overridden

actions.title: Actions
actions.description: Action names this rule applies to; None = any

blast_radius.title: Blast Radius
blast_radius.description: Flags that must match the agent's registered blast radius

RuleWhen.title: RuleWhen
RuleWhen.description:
Conditions ANDed together; omitted fields don't constrain. A rule with
an empty `when` matches everything (useful as a terminal catch-all).

bearerAuth.description: Ingest API key or OIDC JWT. See /authentication.

servers.description: Your Sakshi deployment (single-tenant, in your environment)
